airdrops.about: What is Polymarket? Polymarket is the world’s largest decentralized prediction market, built on the Polygon network. Users buy and sell shares on the outcome of real-world events: elections, sports, crypto prices, geopolitics; with prices reflecting crowd-sourced probabilities. There is no house; trades are peer-to-peer, and smart contracts handle settlement automatically in USDC. The platform became a popular source for real-time outcome probability during the 2024 U.S. elections, processing over $3.6 billion in election-related activity. Polymarket raised $70 million in early fundraising from Founders Fund, General Catalyst, Polychain Capital, Dragonfly Capital, and Vitalik Buterin. Intercontinental Exchange, the parent company of the NYSE, invested $2 billion at a $9 billion valuation in October 2025, consolidating Polymarket’s dominance in the prediction market with over 95% market volume. airdrops.about: What Is Hyperliquid? Hyperliquid is a purpose-built Layer 1 blockchain for high-performance DeFi. At its core is the Hyperliquid DEX: a fully on-chain orderbook perpetuals exchange processing roughly 200,000 orders per second with ~0.2s median latency — no off-chain sequencer, zero gas fees. The platform supports perpetual futures, spot trading, and a native token standard with permissionless liquidity provision. The HYPE token powers the entire ecosystem: staking, governance, fee payments, and token burns. The protocol burns HYPE regularly using a portion of generated revenue — a governance vote in early 2026 approved burning approximately $1 billion worth of HYPE, adding deflationary pressure on top of the staking yield. Since its alpha launch in 2023, Hyperliquid has crossed $100 billion in cumulative trading volume and now commands roughly 60% of the total perpetual DEX market.

airdrops.about: What is Katana? Katana is a blockchain made just for DeFi. Its focused application architecture is meant to provide deeper liquidity and higher yields. Katana doesn’t split liquidity across many protocols. Instead, it channels all activity through one lending protocol (Morpho) and one spot DEX (Sushi), with hundreds of apps building on top of them. This focused approach brings in money from several sources, such as Vault Bridge, sequencer fees, and Agora USD revenue. This money then flows back as rewards across Katana’s main apps. The chain uses Agglayer to make it easy for users to join from other chains and ZK proofs to check state transitions, so users can leave without having to wait long. The KAT token makes the ecosystem work. Users who lock KAT will get vKAT (voting KAT), which is what keeps the chain’s economy going by rewarding people who work together with fees. The vKAT system will go live at the same time as the vKAT Armory staking UI.

airdrops.about: What is Outcome? Outcome is a prediction market platform built natively on Hyperliquid. Users trade binary outcomes on crypto prices, sports events (F1, football), commodities, politics, and finance — all settled on-chain, with no bridging required. Because Outcome runs directly on Hyperliquid, trades execute against your existing Hyperliquid balance at near-zero cost. The platform uses Hyperliquid’s HIP-4 primitive and a custom CLOB matching engine. Markets operate like a real exchange, with bids, asks, and actual price discovery, rather than the AMM-based approach most prediction market protocols use. The project is fully bootstrapped; the team self-funded deployment by staking their own $HYPE tokens, with no VC backing. By mainnet launch in May 2026, Outcome had already processed 500+ market resolutions and logged over 200,000 testnet participants. That’s a real base of activity before any token incentive existed.
